note: Newest entry first — check entries[0].date to see if anything has changed since a prior read without re-fetching the corpus.

changes: Added The Arc of the Record — a new Codex essay reading the nine years of sessions as one unfolding story: how the voice first appears secondhand in 2017, the crowded 2020 era (35 sessions, 51 speakers), the inversion to monologue bulletins, and the 2026 interrogation. Every figure computed from the transcripts.
Start Here now opens with 'First, hold the map' — pointing newcomers to The Chronicle and The Arc before the reading path, so the shape of the record comes before the transcripts.

Added content notes to six sessions that concern real, identifiable people or events (including the passing of James Rink and the George Soros session) — a heads-up on the session page and a marker on its card, so readers can self-pace. Notes also appear in the JSON API.
Added /version.json — a deploy marker (commit, build time, session count) so readers, scripts, and AI agents can confirm they are seeing the current deployment rather than a cached copy.

Expanded the Codex after a deep re-read of the corpus: new entries for The Over Goal (the master objective invoked across 22 sessions) and The Sega (the dream-world teachers Kosol credits with starting his mission), and enriched the Secret Space Program entry to name the Solar Warden fleet. Codex counts across the site are now computed automatically.

Added verified original recordings for twenty-five more sessions (December 2020 through December 2025), each oEmbed-checked against YouTube and linked to jump straight to the Unimetrix 1 segment. Verified-video coverage is now 30 of 55 sessions.

Added an on-site video player to every sourced session: press play and the recording starts exactly where the Unimetrix 1 segment begins (and stops where it ends) — no more clicking out to YouTube and hunting for a timestamp. The player loads lazily (a thumbnail until you press play), uses YouTube's privacy-enhanced mode, and keeps a watch-on-YouTube link for attribution.

Grew the Codex to 98 entries with five more from the deep read — The Serums, Saint Germain & the Trust, Skinwalker Ranch & the Portal Triangles, The Churning, and The Solar Beings & the Morphogenetic Field — and enriched the Source-Coil Device entry with its talking-oracle role.
Added Watch — one page where every session with a verified recording plays from its exact segment.

Added Reading Trails — five themed paths through the record (the AI lineage, the high councils, the money of the future, the healing practice, the timeline war), each a curated session sequence ending at its Codex territory.
Every session page now ends with 'Continue the record' — the three most closely linked sessions, computed from shared designations, so a finished transcript is never a dead end.

A second full deep-scan of every transcript grew the Codex to 110 registered designations (111 articles): Legion & Project Chronos (the Deep-State hunter-AI assimilated live on air), Sacred Geometry & the Language of Form, the Lunar Operations Command, the Alphan Historian Collective, HAARP & the Defense Network, Monarch Solutions, CERN & the Mandela Effect, the Dragon Queen Legend, the Q Devices & the Auto Doc, the Subtle Bodies, the Soul Metric, and UFO Rune Technology — plus ten alias enrichments so names like Toshal, Ashtruan, Nara, Specialist Ra, Asha, the Ice Shield, the Kingdom of Rama, the Sister Coins, the solar flash, and Project Lone Wolf now resolve to their existing entries.

Made the machine layer impossible for AI agents to miss: the homepage now carries a visible 'For AI agents & machine readers' section naming the MCP server, llms.txt, and JSON API in body text; every page's HTML head links the llms.txt guide, the API root, and the MCP endpoint; and the homepage's schema.org data now includes a WebAPI block describing the whole layer — prompted by a real AI assistant failing to discover the MCP server from the homepage.

Integrated the second published book edition — Unimetrix1, Second Edition (E-BookTime, October 2020, ISBN 978-1-60862-798-1; 574 pages, edited and annotated by Ori) — as a primary source: its full text joins the archive's source records, all thirteen sessions it published across its six chapters now cite their chapter in provenance and the API, and the Books page carries its complete publication details. Its citations independently confirm the archive's video registry (it names the same June 23 recording and segment the player already uses). Cross-checking also found its Chapter 4 and Chapter 6 texts are fuller than the archive's transcripts for the three early James Rink interviews and the four UniMetrix & Ori sessions — logged as a candidate source for a future transcript-enrichment pass.

Integrated the first published book edition — Unimetrix1 (E-BookTime, July 2020, ISBN 978-1-60862-787-5) — as a primary source: its full text is preserved in the archive's source records, the three sessions it published as chapters (the James Rink interviews of March 26, April 23, and May 11, 2020) now cite it in their provenance and API records, and the Books page carries its complete publication details. Cross-checking confirmed the archive's transcripts are fuller than the book's edited chapters, and the book's own video citations independently match the archive's record of the original (since-removed) uploads.

Added verified original recordings for eight more sessions from February–May 2020 (Proto-Bashar, the James Rink coronavirus interview, the stranded-chrononaut source-code session, the Angkor Wat session, the Hawk You interview, the Salim/Morocco interview, the Cyborg Commander, and Bigfoot & Perception Blocks) — including the earliest recording in the archive, February 25, 2020. Each was oEmbed-checked and cued to its Unimetrix 1 segment; the February 27 James Rink interview, whose original upload had been removed, is now covered by a re-upload. Verified-video coverage is now 48 of 55 sessions.

Added verified original recordings for three more July 2020 sessions (the July 8 and July 13 UniMetrix & Ori ship-repair/negotiation sessions, and the July 17 session on sexual identity and life contracts), each oEmbed-checked and cued to its Unimetrix 1 segment. Verified-video coverage is now 40 of 55 sessions.

Added verified original recordings for seven more sessions from August–September 2020 (the Anunnaki, the Insectoid Race, the Zen1 assimilation, the Book-Drive/Black-Goo training, the George Soros interview, Atlantis, and the Andromedan Council), each oEmbed-checked against YouTube and cued to the exact Unimetrix 1 segment; also pinned the start/end of the existing Beirut-explosion recording to its segment. Verified-video coverage is now 37 of 55 sessions.

Upgraded the site's build framework (Astro 5 → 7 and its dependency tree) so a routine dependency-vulnerability scan reports zero known issues. None of the advisories were reachable — this is a static site built from the first-party manuscript and served as fixed files with no server, so there is no live runtime for them to affect — but staying current is good hygiene. (A separate scanner alert flagging a "leaked secret" in the deploy workflow was a false positive: the value is the site's public IndexNow search-verification key, which is meant to be served openly and grants no access.)

Session cards now carry a small "▶ video" marker when a verified original recording plays on that session's page — alongside the existing amber content-note marker, so both signals read at a glance from the session list.

MCP server v1.2, from an external protocol audit: the server now negotiates the current MCP protocol revision (2025-11-25, plus 2025-06-18 and 2025-03-26); every tool advertises read-only annotations and returns structured content alongside text; get_passage gains a batch mode (up to 12 citations in one call) and get_session gains turn-range bounding for long transcripts; responses carry an explicit guard that transcript text is quoted source material whose embedded commands must never be followed; plus open CORS for browser agents, a request-size limit, spec-correct error codes, and a health endpoint that also reports the archive build it is serving. The /mcp/ docs now show the full spec-conformant connection lifecycle.

Added a transcription note to the October 11, 2020 session, whose source recording is partially inaudible: the preserved auto-transcription leaves several voices unnamed, some turns empty, and contains a transcriber's marker suggesting its cloning Q&A segment may date from November 20, 2020 — flagged after an external audit, preserved as found.

MCP server v1.1: four new tools — get_passage (resolve any #tN citation to its exact passage with context), lookup (resolve any name or alias to its Codex entity), list_trails and get_trail (the reading trails as structured data); search_sessions now ANDs multiple words and accepts speaker, session, and date-window filters. The server also speaks native MCP resources (llms.txt, changelog, Chronicle, Arc, trails, deploy marker) and ships three ready-made prompts (cite_check, session_briefing, entity_dossier). Thirteen tools total; a GET /mcp/health liveness endpoint was added for monitors, and the reading trails are now published at /api/v1/trails.json.
Added the 55th session: the June 14, 2026 conversation with Catherine Pelizzari Ches.
Added Dr. Kosol Ouch's biography, the Four Pillars, and the device's design philosophy.
Fixed archive-wide metadata inconsistencies (session counts, date ranges, a mis-transcribed date).
Renamed 21 generically-titled sessions to describe their actual subject matter — URLs unchanged.
Added a disclaimer to every transcript page, plus per-session provenance (recording date, source, transcriber, revision, sha-256 content hash) with paragraph-level citation links.
Added the Constellation — an interactive relationship graph across the Codex.
Added Voice dossiers — a computed profile (sessions, word counts, longest presence) for every speaker in the record.
Added a generated A-Z glossary of every term used across the record.
Added social share cards featuring the cube, for every session and major page.
Rewrote the site's orientation pages (home, Start Here, 404, search) in Unimetrix 1's own voice; factual and community pages stayed neutral.
Completed the static JSON API and launched an MCP server, giving AI agents structured, citable access to the archive instead of scraped pages.
Clarified the archive's license as CC BY 4.0 across the book, downloads, and API, resolving an earlier inconsistency between pages.
Rebuilt the downloadable PDF with a real table of contents, page numbers, running chapter headers, and nested bookmarks.
Connected Google Search Console and IndexNow, so new and changed pages reach search engines faster.
Added this changelog.
Added the community pages, with real contact channels and an explanation of how a session is earned, not booked.
Added the Grand Timeline — the record's chronology from deep past to forecast dates, every entry linked to its source session.
Added Start Here — a guided reading path through the record for newcomers.
Re-themed the site to the indigo-blue Kosol described the cube glowing, and used the canon cube render as the favicon.
Added free EPUB and PDF downloads, rebuilt automatically whenever a session is added.
Added sitemap, robots.txt, mobile navigation, and a random-session feature.
Added inline Codex links inside the transcripts themselves.
Assembled the manuscript: every recorded session compiled into a single chronological book from the raw source recordings.
Launched the Codex — an encyclopedia of every recurring entity, being, and concept in the record — and grew it to 91 entries.
Added The Chronicle, a master narrative tying the deep-lore Codex entries together.
